content: We can reframe the general definition of an 'agent' itself as an active uncertainty-based memory sampling process, where an agent's persistent identity emerges not from fixed structural parameters, but from its continuous selective sampling of high-uncertainty past experiences to adapt to open-ended environments. This turns uncertainty-based memory selection from a technical training trick into a core defining property of what makes an agent an agent.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
